data.narrative:
Reflex Aerospace is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Walter Ballheimer, Alexander Genzel, and Christian Lindener, having raised €50M+. The valuation universe comprises 13 comparable companies across the Space & Aerospace sector: 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, Planet Labs, Impulse Space, and Apex Space.

Standard triangulation approaches face material constraints. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within narrow filters—only 2 adjacent-stage peers identified when 3 are required for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring cannot be executed because Reflex Aerospace's revenue is not disclosed; while the Space & Aerospace public median trades at 25.8x price-to-sales across the 6-ticker universe, applying this multiple requires the target's top-line figure. The broader private peer set (SpaceX, Sierra Space, et al.) operates at disparate stages and valuation tiers, limiting direct calibration.

Given these constraints, a quantitatively grounded range cannot be derived from the available data. Valuation would require either: disclosure of Reflex Aerospace's revenue (enabling public-comp application), or identification of additional same-stage private comparables with disclosed valuations. Management should be prompted to provide revenue guidance or comparable transaction data to support a defensible estimate.
